other_observers: Mark Peterson, Jack Peterson; Mark Peterson found and identified this bird from close range.
description: I saw this bird from a distance perched and in flight but I didn't have a chance to study it before it flew. I asked Mark Peterson who found the bird and digiscoped it if I could submit his photograph as most of the record. Therefore the information provided here is mostly what I see in the photograph plus a little of what I saw when the bird flew.  Small, slim, short-bodied jeager, pale morph. Cold coloration. Head very light gray contrasting with slightly darker breast band and paler belly. Dorsally two-toned with gray back and darker gray primaires (seen in flight and perched). Bill two-toned on a small head. Strong barring on distal third of ventral surface.   In flight wings were slender. I did not see any wing flashes but I was using binoculars and not a spotting scope.Behaviors: Perched in very shallow water where midges were hatching and upon which it was feeding. In flight it flew directly away at a fast clip and disappeared in the direction of the Antero Reservoir Dam.Call: nonePlumage: juvenile
